name: Canning Okra
recipeIngredient: 11 pounds okra for a canner load yielding 7 quarts
7 pounds okra for a canner load yielding 9 pints
1 bushel okra (26 pounds) yields approximately 16-18 quarts, or about 1.5 pounds per quart
recipeInstructions:
1. Select young, tender pods and discard any diseased or rust-spotted ones.
2. Wash the pods thoroughly and trim the ends.
3. Leave the pods whole or cut them into 1-inch pieces.
4. Place the okra in a saucepan with hot water and bring to a boil for 2 minutes.
5. Drain the okra and fill jars with hot okra and cooking liquid, leaving 1-inch headspace.
6. Add 1 teaspoon of salt per quart, if desired.
7. Adjust lids and process according to Table 1 or Table 2 based on the canning method used.
Table 1: Recommended processing time for hot-packed okra in a dial-gauge pressure canner
- Pints: 25 minutes
- Quarts: 40 minutes
- Canner pressure (PSI) at altitudes of 0-2000 ft: 11 lb; 2001-4000 ft: 12 lb; 4001-6000 ft: 13 lb; 6001-8000 ft: 14 lb
Table 2: Recommended processing time for hot-packed okra in a weighted-gauge pressure canner
- Pints: 25 minutes
- Quarts: 40 minutes
- Canner pressure (PSI) at altitudes of 0-1000 ft: 10 lb; above 1000 ft: 15 lb
